vehicle.transmission: 6-speed automatic with Touchtronic paddle-shift manual mode

label: Money Pit
verdict: Structurally damaged history makes this a risky purchase despite its allure.
whatMakesSpecial: The DB9 is a quintessential grand tourer with a hand-built naturally aspirated V12, offering a driving experience that modern turbocharged engines can't replicate.
whyEnthusiastsCare: Aston Martin's DB9 is celebrated for its timeless design and the symphonic V12, representing an era of luxury grand tourers that are becoming increasingly rare.

ownerVibe.label: Dealer / Trade Listing
ownerVibe.reasoning: Listed by a dealership on behalf of a private seller, with professional sales language and finance options.

carsCoffee.description: Aston Martins always draw a crowd, and a DB9 is no exception with its elegant design and V12 soundtrack.

communityCredibility.description: The DB9 is respected among enthusiasts for its design and performance, though the damaged history may dampen some of its appeal.

socialStanding: The car that turns heads and makes people wonder if you're a secret agent.

specSignificance.note: Naturally aspirated V12 engines are increasingly rare and highly sought after.
Offers a blend of automatic convenience and manual control.

priceVerdict.reason: Without a listed price, it's impossible to judge value; however, the structural damage history significantly impacts its worth.

reasons: Aston Martin badge and V12 engine command a premium despite the damage history.

priceOutlook.reason: Structural damage history will deter collectors and depress resale value.

issues.title: Structural Damage History
issues.detail: Significant risk of hidden issues and reduced resale value; repairs may not have restored original integrity.

issues.title: V12 Maintenance Costs
issues.detail: Routine servicing and potential repairs can be exorbitant, with parts often needing to be sourced from overseas.

issues.title: Touchtronic Transmission
issues.detail: Known for occasional electronic issues, with repairs costing upwards of $5,000 NZD.

steeringFeel.description: The steering is precise and well-weighted, offering excellent feedback that enhances the grand touring experience.

engineCharacter.description: The V12 delivers a rich, sonorous soundtrack with linear power delivery that builds smoothly to redline, a hallmark of classic Aston Martin performance.

dailyComfort.description: While the ride is comfortable for long journeys, the low seating position and limited rear visibility can make daily driving challenging.

overallFun.description: The combination of V12 power, elegant design, and grand touring capability makes it a joy to drive, especially on open roads.

drivingCharacter.summary: A thrilling grand tourer with a V12 soundtrack that elevates any drive.

reasons: V12 engines are becoming rare, but structural damage limits collectibility.

regretRisk.reason: The structural damage history and high maintenance costs could lead to buyer's remorse.

marketTrend.reason: Damage history and high running costs will limit appeal and depress values.

reasons: Structural damage history severely impacts resale value.
V12 maintenance and repair costs are extremely high.
Potential for significant depreciation due to damage history.

redFlags.explanation: This car has been recorded as structurally damaged, indicating a previous write-off or insurance total loss â request a full PPSR report before proceeding.

modPotential.collectorRisk: Modifying a DB9, especially one with a damage history, will further reduce its already limited resale value.

questionsToAsk: Can you provide the full damage and repair history?
Has the Touchtronic transmission received any recent servicing?
Are there any outstanding issues from the structural damage?
enthusiastTake: While the DB9 is a stunning grand tourer with a V12 heart, the structural damage history is a major concern that could lead to significant financial headaches.

alternatives.name: Jaguar XKR (X150)
alternatives.whySuited: Offers a similar British grand tourer experience with a supercharged V8.
alternatives.howDiffers: The XKR is generally more reliable and less expensive to maintain than the Aston.

alternatives.name: BMW E63 M6
alternatives.whySuited: Provides a V10 experience with similar grand touring capabilities.
alternatives.howDiffers: The M6's V10 is high-revving and more aggressive, but maintenance is similarly costly.

alternatives.name: Maserati GranTurismo
alternatives.whySuited: Another Italian grand tourer with a sonorous V8 and stunning design.
alternatives.howDiffers: The GranTurismo offers a more exotic flair but also comes with high maintenance costs.
